DATA REVEALS CITY WORKERS' BONUS PAY

Matt Stiles Houston Chronicle January/February 2007
Government & PoliticsInvestigative MethodsData & Databases

The story uncovers a payroll-padding scandal in Houston's city council's administrative office, revealing that four employees improperly received $143,000 in bonus payments. Further analysis showed many city employees received bonus payments that were not initially disclosed.

After attending a computer-assisted reporting Boot Camp, Matt Stiles returned to tackle a payroll-padding scandal involving city employees. Data analysis revealed extensive bonus payments totaling around $4 million, contradicting city officials' claims of rarity. Detailed payroll data and employee interviews highlighted systemic issues, prompting citywide investigations and further reporting on potential misuse of incentive systems by city mechanics and staff.
